title: gasteranthus rupestris
intro: Gasteranthus rupestris, also known as the rock bellflower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Borgeaceae. This plant is native to the Andes mountains in South America, where it grows in shaded, rocky areas. Traditionally, Gasteranthus rupestris is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas and infusions, to help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues.
